items.name: Army Combat Uniform (ACU/OCP)
items.notes: Must meet current Army uniform standards
items.whyNeeded: Worn throughout the course for all training events.
items.selectionTips: Bring at least 3 sets. Ensure they are serviceable and meet current standards.

items.name: Combat Boots
items.whyNeeded: You will walk miles daily on varied terrain. Foot care is critical.
items.selectionTips: Break in boots before arrival. Leather or approved synthetic. Consider a second pair to rotate.

recommendedProducts.name: Danner Reckoning 8" Boot
recommendedProducts.reason: Field-tested by soldiers worldwide. Superior ankle support and waterproofing hold up through ruck marches and full rappelling days.

recommendedProducts.name: Belleville 790ST Steel Toe
recommendedProducts.reason: Military-grade construction at a budget price. Rated for sustained heavy use on concrete and rough terrain.

items.name: Rucksack (ALICE or MOLLE)
items.whyNeeded: Required for ruck marches and carrying field equipment.
items.selectionTips: Must have a frame. Pack smart — weight distribution matters for long ruck marches.

recommendedProducts.name: Mystery Ranch NICE Frame Pack
recommendedProducts.reason: Preferred by SOF operators. The NICE frame transfers load efficiently, protecting your lower back across multi-mile movements.

items.name: Camelback / Hydration System
items.whyNeeded: Staying hydrated is critical during intense physical training.
items.selectionTips: Bring extra water purification tablets as backup.

recommendedProducts.name: CamelBak BFM 3L Hydration Pack
recommendedProducts.reason: Army-issue for a reason — hands-free 3L hydration with a bladder that survives years of field abuse without leaking.

items.name: PT Uniform
items.whyNeeded: Required for morning PT and physical fitness tests.
items.selectionTips: Current Army PT uniform only. No civilian workout gear.

items.name: Running Shoes
items.whyNeeded: PT runs require proper footwear to prevent injury.
items.selectionTips: Use road runners for asphalt, trail shoes for terrain. Prioritize comfort over looks.

recommendedProducts.name: Brooks Ghost 15 Running Shoe
recommendedProducts.reason: Consistently rated #1 for military PT. Exceptional cushioning reduces injury risk across daily 2–5 mile runs on pavement.

items.name: Gloves (Leather Work Gloves)
items.whyNeeded: Required for rappelling operations. Protects hands from rope burns.
items.selectionTips: Leather gloves only — synthetic can melt on a hot rope. Bring extras.

recommendedProducts.name: Wells Lamont Leather Work Gloves
recommendedProducts.reason: The standard Air Assault rappelling glove. Thick cowhide leather won't melt or slip on a loaded rope like synthetics do.

items.name: Notepad and Pens
items.whyNeeded: Must take notes during all classroom instruction.
items.selectionTips: Rite in the Rain notepads are ideal — waterproof in the field.

recommendedProducts.name: Rite in the Rain All-Weather Notebook
recommendedProducts.reason: Waterproof pages survive rain, sweat, and humidity. A special operations standard for decades — your notes survive whatever the course throws at them.

items.name: Dog Tags
items.whyNeeded: Required identification for all military personnel.
items.selectionTips: Must be current and legible. Bring both primary and duplicate.

items.name: Military ID Card
items.selectionTips: Do not lose this. Keep it secure at all times.

items.name: Moleskin / Foot Care Kit
items.whyNeeded: Blisters are a top reason soldiers quit. Treat them immediately.
items.selectionTips: Bring moleskin, athletic tape, and foot powder. Consider Leukotape for severe blister-prone areas.

recommendedProducts.name: Dr. Scholl's Moleskin Plus
recommendedProducts.reason: Cuts directly to any shape and adheres firmly even when wet. The fastest, simplest blister treatment you can pack.

items.name: Body Glide Anti-Chafe
items.description: Anti-chafing balm for long ruck marches
items.selectionTips: Apply to inner thighs, underarms, and anywhere uniform rubs.

recommendedProducts.name: Body Glide Original Anti-Chafe Balm
recommendedProducts.reason: Prevents raw skin on ruck marches better than petroleum jelly. Odorless, won't stain your uniform, and lasts all day.

items.name: Mini Flashlight / Headlamp
items.selectionTips: Red lens preserves night vision. Essential for early morning and night operations.

recommendedProducts.name: Petzl Tactikka Core Headlamp
recommendedProducts.reason: Red and white output modes with 300-lumen peak brightness. USB rechargeable — no scrambling for batteries during the course.

items.name: Knee Sleeves / Braces
items.description: Compression knee sleeves for ruck marches
items.selectionTips: Great for injury prevention if you have any prior knee issues.

items.name: Protein / Energy Bars
items.selectionTips: Pack bars that won't melt. Clif bars and RXBars work well in the field.

items.name: Civilian Clothing (Off-Duty Areas Only)
items.notes: Civilian clothes allowed in barracks during off-hours, not in training areas

items.name: Alcohol
items.notes: Zero tolerance. Can result in course dismissal.

items.name: Non-Military Rucksack
items.notes: Must use military-spec rucksack with frame for ruck marches.
